45.reason: [Loop runs 'n' times after already reading one item, so total items read = n+1 instead of n]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop runs 'n' times after already reading one item, so total items read = n+1 instead of n]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This loop runs 'n' times after already reading one item, resulting in reading 'n+1' items total instead of 'n'. It should run 'n-1' times or read all 'n' items in a consistent loop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop runs n times but first item already read, so total items processed = n+1, causing extra input read and potential undefined behavior]

47.reason: [Return value is assigned to 'q' but 'root' is not updated; subsequent insertions don't affect the tree rooted at 'root']

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Return value is assigned to 'q' but 'root' is not updated; subsequent insertions don't affect the tree rooted at 'root']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The returned root from createBST is ignored; insertion into BST may fail to update the tree correctly when inserting into an empty subtree that is not the global root. However, since 'root' is non-NULL after first insertion, this only affects subtrees. But more critically, the return value should be assigned to maintain correct structure, e.g., root = createBST(root, item) in a proper loop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Return value ignored; tree structure not updated correctly for recursive insertions after the first node]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The return value of createBST is assigned to 'q', but 'root' is never updated for subsequent insertions, so only the first insertion affects the tree. All later insertions are lost because 'root' remains unchanged.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The returned pointer 'q' is unused; the tree is not updated correctly because 'createBST' returns the (possibly new) root of the subtree, but the result is not assigned back to root or the appropriate child pointer in the iterative context. However, since it's called recursively in a functional style, the real issue is that the first item is read outside the loop and then loop runs 'n' more times, leading to n+1 total insertions.]
